factors.name: US Labor Market Weakness (NFP)
factors.summary: Thursday's softer-than-expected nonfarm payrolls data has reduced Fed rate-hike expectations, particularly for September, lowering near-term real yields. This is typically supportive for long-duration growth stocks like mega-cap tech.

factors.name: Fed Policy Repricing
factors.summary: Market now pricing ~50% odds of a November rate hike (vs higher expectations prior), and doubts on September hikes have deepened. Lower real yields benefit NDX-heavy tech names with long cash-flow durations.

factors.name: Macro Momentum & Risk-On
factors.summary: Global stocks hit historic all-time highs pre-holiday; Bitcoin at $62.3K; AUD, GBP, NZD, EUR all strengthening on USD weakness post-NFP. Risk appetite intact despite heat wave/power grid tensions.

factors.name: US Power Grid Stress & Heat Wave
factors.summary: Extreme heat threatening US power grids and forcing emergency curbs; could disrupt Q3 earnings/operations and cap upside momentum into late summer. Electricity supply risk may weigh on data center / AI capex near-term.

factors.name: Geopolitical Calm (Iran-US Deal)
factors.summary: OPEC output surge, Strait of Hormuz reopening, and US-Iran talks reducing energy shock fears. Lower oil (~$68.65 WTI) reduces inflation pressure and supports equities; gold weakness also aligns with lower real yields benefiting equities.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Window
factors.summary: No major FAANG earnings in news window; Q2 reporting season largely concluded. Next catalysts are earnings guidance/conference calls and capital allocation announcements (e.g., CPP's $1.75B EQT AI infrastructure deal signals continued capex appetite).

factors.name: AI & Semiconductor Sentiment
factors.summary: Modest AI bullishness (Kleiner Perkins VC outlook, robotics hype) but no major semiconductor supply or demand shocks; Zcash/crypto noise unrelated to NDX.

factors.name: USD Weakness Post-NFP
factors.summary: USD/JPY, USD/CHF, USD/CNH all weakening on softer labor data. Weaker dollar reduces headwinds for NQ international revenues and typically supports equity multiples.

factors.name: Existing Move (4h: +0.44%, 24h: -1.24%)
factors.summary: NAS100 flat to modestly positive in quiet US holiday trading (Independence Day). Bounce from -1.24% 24h low is underway but move is small and lacks conviction; holiday liquidity is thin.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: Softer NFP should push real yields lower (5Y TIPS expected to compress), directly bullish for long-duration growth; no competing inflation surprise to extend nominal yields sharply higher.

screening.narrative: NAS100 opened Q3 mixed after a blockbuster H1, with Thursday's weaker-than-expected NFP data triggering a dovish repricing of Fed policy (Sept hike odds now ~50% vs prior bullish bets). This has supported equities by pushing real yields lower, benefiting long-duration mega-cap tech. Geopolitical risks have eased (Iran-US deal, OPEC output surge) and global risk appetite remains intact at all-time highs. However, the move is unfolding in thin, holiday-shortened US trading, and a US power grid stress event from an extreme heat wave adds a near-term operational wildcard. The bias is cautiously bullish on fundamentals (lower real yields, dovish pivot) but tempered by phase-2 consolidation and lack of fresh mega-cap catalysts.
catalysts_ahead: Fed Minutes (likely early–mid week, will clarify Sept hike intentions)
Mega-cap earnings guidance / capital allocation (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL guidance windows vary; check earnings calendar)
10Y Treasury auction (next week; will test real yield floor)
CPI / PCE inflation data (mid-month; biggest NDX driver if surprise occurs)
FOMC meeting (likely July 29–30; will reset rate expectations)
screening.risk_warning: If another hot inflation print (CPI, PCE) arrives mid-month, or if Fed speakers push back on dovish repricing, real yields could re-steepen and crush NDX multiples. Power grid failures or broader macro disruption from heat wave could trigger liquidity crisis / circuit breakers. Also: thin holiday liquidity can reverse moves sharply; watch for whipsaw into re-open (Tuesday).
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early phase-2 consolidation; avoid chasing up into resistance 29,450–29,600. Wait for retest of 29,100–29,150 support zone with bullish divergence (lower lows on volume) or break above 29,600 with conviction before committing. Holiday liquidity is thin—size down and expect slippage.
